level_signals: 10+ years of progressive experience in revenue operations
5+ years leading operations functions at senior manager, director, or VP level
Manages three direct reports each with established teams (manager-of-managers scope)
Reports to SVP & Head of AI Operations
Represents revenue operations at the executive table and in board-level operational narrative
Comp range $190K–$220K base, consistent with VP-level at high-growth tech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of progressive experience in revenue operations, sales operations, or business operations at a SaaS, marketplace, or technology company
5+ years leading operations functions at the senior manager, director, or VP level, including experience managing other managers and directors (manager-of-managers scope)
Prior experience as a VP or senior-most operations leader at a high-growth technology company, or clear readiness to step into that level of responsibility
Deep expertise across the full RevOps stack: pipeline management, forecasting, territory design, lead routing, renewal operations, and marketing operations
Experience in revenue enablement: building or overseeing the programs, content, and operational support that make go-to-market teams effective
Proven experience evaluating, implementing, and rationalizing technology platforms. You've made hard calls about what to consolidate, migrate, or retire
Hands-on proficiency with Salesforce at an advanced level; experience with HubSpot, marketing automation, and sales engagement platforms

Demonstrated use of AI tools in your operational work: concrete examples of building workflows, automations, or analyses using AI (Claude, ChatGPT, agent frameworks, or equivalent), beyond theoretical interest
Strong financial acumen. Comfortable with bookings forecasting, ARR/MRR modeling, and unit economics
Experience operating across multiple segments (enterprise, mid-market, SMB/growth) and multiple GTM motions
Executive presence and exceptional stakeholder management across C-suite, board, sales, marketing, and services teams
A bias for action and comfort with ambiguity in a high-growth, fast-evolving environment

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in influencer marketing, creator economy, marketplace, or affiliate/performance marketing
Familiarity with creator-side operations (HubSpot, creator lifecycle management, GMV attribution)
Track record of building or scaling a Dev & Integrations or systems team within an operations function
Experience with AI automation platforms (Gumloop, Zapier AI, or equivalent) and building agent-assisted operational workflows
Background operating in a PE-backed or pre-IPO environment with board-level reporting expectations
Experience with data enrichment and prospecting tools (Clay, ZoomInfo, or equivalent)

scope.team_size_estimate: 3 direct reports (Principal RevOps Manager, Director of Creator Operations, Head of Sales Enablement), each with established teams across 6 sub-functions
scope.surface: End-to-end Revenue Operations: Sales Ops, Marketing Ops, Customer Ops, Creator Ops, Enablement, and Dev & Integrations
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS + managed services; influencer/creator marketplace; enterprise GTM motion
core_responsibilities: Be accountable for the end-to-end operational infrastructure across Sales Ops, Marketing Ops, Customer Ops, Creator Ops, Enablement, and Dev & Integrations
Own outcomes for pipeline mechanics, forecasting, territory design and assignment, lead routing, and deal operations across enterprise, mid-market, and growth segments
Drive accountability for renewal and expansion operations across the client lifecycle
Establish and maintain the operational cadence (weekly performance reviews, monthly business reviews, quarterly planning cycles)
Serve as the senior operational partner to the CRO, CMO, CFO, and cross-functional leadership on go-to-market execution
Be accountable for the tech stack (Salesforce, HubSpot, Clay, ChiliPiper, Nooks, Chorus, and more)
Drive adoption of AI-powered workflows, automations, and agent-assisted processes
Manage and develop three direct reports
Develop and own a forward-looking operations roadmap that aligns to company OKRs and the broader AI transformation strategy

green_flags: Clear reporting structure (reports to SVP & Head of AI Operations)
Specific tech stack named (Salesforce, HubSpot, Clay, ChiliPiper, Nooks, Chorus), indicating operational maturity
Explicit team structure described (3 direct reports, 6 sub-functions)
AI transformation mandate is concrete, not vague
Company has strong enterprise client base (Nike, Wayfair, Unilever, Southwest Airlines)
Remote-eligible for select positions (hybrid/remote both tagged)
red_flags: JD tags both #LI-Hybrid and #LI-Remote, creating ambiguity about whether this role is truly remote-eligible or hybrid; body says 'for select positions, we are open to hiring fully remote candidates' without confirming this role qualifies
Equity not mentioned despite VP-level seniority, which is unusual for high-growth tech
Very broad scope (6 sub-functions, end-to-end RevOps, AI transformation mandate) could signal under-resourcing or organizational complexity
